news.title: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon in annual letter cites risks in geopolitics, AI and private markets
news.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon calls for recommitment to U.S. ideals in annual letter to shareholders. Dimon noted the country's 250th anniversary and called for a broad recommitting to American ideals.

news.title: Trump's Iran ultimatum and signals of a possible deal keep investors on tenterhooks
news.summary: Investors are scrambling to position for binary outcomes in the Iran conflict. An imminent deal or further escalation could be the most consequential week of the conflict.

news.title: India turns to Iran for oil and gas after 7-year hiatus, signaling limits to U.S. tilt
news.summary: India has resumed oil and gas imports from Iran after seven years. The move is a bid to rebalance its ties with Tehran and secure supplies through the Strait of Hormuz.

news.title: Warsh nomination moves ahead, putting Trump's competing Fed plans on a collision course
news.summary: A Senate committee will consider Kevin Warsh's nomination even as one of its members still plans to block it. Warsh is a former head of the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. He was nominated by President Barack Obama.

news.title: 'Silent killers': How AI start-ups are trying to solve one of the retail industry's biggest problems
news.summary: The rapid evolution of generative AI has finally made these applications good enough to meaningfully impact retailers' bottom lines. It is now possible to use AI to predict what customers want to buy and when.

news.title: Jamie Dimon warns rising oil prices could trigger a recession and a bear market in 2026
news.summary: Jamie Dimon warns of a “skunk at the party” in 2026 in the form of rising inflation leading to a selloff in the stock market.

news.title: ‘I worked very hard’: I’m 71 and have $6 million after scrimping and saving. My son, 33, wants money for a house. Do I say yes?
news.summary: “My son is 33 years old, married, with an 18-month-old baby and another baby due in September,” she said.

news.title: Multimillion-dollar CEO pay at these 20 low-wage companies is costing you. This is the only fix.
news.summary: CEO pay at the ‘low-wage 20’ averages $18.6 million. Many of their median workers rely on taxpayer-supported Medicaid and SNAP.

news.title: April is usually a strong month for stocks — but three factors now jeopardize the market rebound
news.summary: Worries about Fed rate hikes and souring earnings expectations could easily trip up the market for a second straight month. The Dow Jones Industrial Average is down 0.7% for the month.

news.title: U.S. oil prices stay above $110 a barrel after Iran pushes back against Trump’s deadline for Tehran to reopen Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: Trump has threatened to knock out Iran’s power plants and bridges if the crucial strait is not reopened. The U.S. president has also said he will close the Strait of Hormuz if it is not re-opened.
